---
title: CLI
---

# Basic usage

Render a `.qmd` file to HTML (the default format):

```bash
calepin document.qmd
```

This produces `document.html` in the same directory.


# Output formats

*Calepin* supports four rendering engines: `html`, `latex`, `typst`, and `markdown`. Select one with `-t`:

```bash
calepin document.qmd -t latex    # → document.tex
calepin document.qmd -t typst    # → document.typ
calepin document.qmd -t markdown # → document.md
```

You can also set the target in the YAML front matter:

```yaml
---
target: latex
---
```

Or let *Calepin* infer it from the output file extension with `-o`:

```bash
calepin document.qmd -o paper.tex
```


# Multiple formats

Render to several formats at once by passing comma-separated target names:

```bash
calepin render document.qmd -t html,latex,typst
```

Each target produces its own output file. Code chunks execute once per unique figure format (e.g., SVG for HTML, PDF for LaTeX), and the cache distinguishes them automatically.


# PDF compilation

The `pdf` target renders to LaTeX and compiles to PDF:

```bash
calepin render document.qmd -t pdf
```

To compile via Typst instead, override the engine:

```bash
calepin render document.qmd -t pdf --engine typst
```

The `--engine` flag (previously `--base`) selects which rendering engine a compound target uses. It is only valid for `pdf` and `book` targets.


# Themes

Apply a theme on top of the target to change the look of your output:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --theme blog
calepin document.qmd -t website --theme blog
```

Themes are directories under `_calepin/themes/` that bundle templates, snippets, and assets. See [Themes](templates/themes.html) for details.


# Live preview

The `--preview` flag starts a local server, opens your browser, and live-reloads whenever you save the file:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --preview
```

This serves the rendered HTML at `http://localhost:3456` by default. Use `--port` to change the port:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --preview --port 8080
```

For LaTeX and Typst, preview compiles to PDF and reloads the PDF viewer on each save.

Press `Ctrl+C` to stop the preview server.


# Metadata overrides

The `--set` flag overrides YAML front matter values from the command line. This is useful for toggling options without editing the file:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --set toc=true
calepin document.qmd --set title="Draft" toc=false number-sections=true
```

Multiple key-value pairs can follow a single `--set`:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --set bibliography=refs.bib fig-width=6
```


# Quiet mode

Suppress progress messages:

```bash
calepin document.qmd -q
```


# Examples

```bash
# Render to HTML with table of contents
calepin paper.qmd --set toc=true

# Render to PDF via Typst
calepin paper.qmd -t pdf --engine typst

# Render with a theme
calepin paper.qmd -t website --theme blog

# Live preview while writing
calepin paper.qmd --preview
```
